sentence: In dem Augenblick verschwand das Schloß, und sie saßen wieder in der alten Hütte. „Nun hast dus,“ sagte der Mann, „jetzt können wir wieder am Hungertuch nagen.“ „Ach,“ sprach die Frau, „ich will den Reichthum lieber nicht, wenn ich nicht weiß von wem er kommt, sonst habe ich doch keine Ruhe.“

sentence: Ich habe mich krank gemeldet, weil ich gestern Abend einen über den Durst getrunken habe.

However, in German, it is an idiom that means to have drunk too much alcohol, to have had one too many. The sentence uses this phrase figuratively to explain why the speaker called in sick, implying they were hungover due to excessive drinking the previous night. Therefore, the usage is idiomatic.

sentence: Ich begriff 1963 die Bundesrepublik weder als Zünglein an der Waage noch als amerikanischen Gegenpol, noch als Speerspitze des Kalten Krieges, sondern fand, daß die Bundesrepublik ihren eigenen Part im Konzert des Westens spielen und ihren eigenen Beitrag leisten müsse - jener Verantwortung gemäß, die sie habe und die ihr niemand abnehme.

args.explanation:

2. "Speerspitze des Kalten Krieges": This idiom refers to the leading or most advanced force in a conflict. Here, it is used metaphorically to describe the Federal Republic's role in the Cold War, not a literal spearhead.
3. "im Konzert des Westens": This idiom signifies acting in coordination or harmony with other Western nations. It is used figuratively to express the idea of the Federal Republic playing its part among Western countries, not literally performing in a musical concert.

In this sentence, it is used figuratively to express Jean Brodie's hope that Sandy and Rose will belong to the best or the elite in the future. "Eines Tages" literally means "one day" and is used here in its literal sense, referring to a future point in time.

args.explanation: The phrase "crème de la crème" is a French expression that literally translates to "cream of the cream." In English, and as used in this German sentence, it is an idiom that refers to the very best of a group or class of people or things. It is used metaphorically to denote excellence and superiority, not the literal meaning of cream.
